name: Notion API
what_it_does: Notion's workspace API for reading and writing pages, databases, blocks, and users in Notion workspaces, with an official MCP server for AI agent integration.
use_cases: Reading and querying Notion databases to retrieve structured data for agents
Creating and updating Notion pages and database records from agent workflows
Building AI-powered knowledge base search and retrieval
Automating documentation generation and publishing to Notion
Syncing project management data between Notion and other tools
not_for: Real-time collaborative editing (API is not real-time)
High-frequency bulk data operations (rate limits are tight)
Binary file storage (Notion is not a file storage service)
best_when: An agent needs to read from or write to Notion workspaces as a knowledge base or project management system, especially via the official MCP server.
avoid_when: You need high-throughput data operations or real-time collaboration features.

auth.notes: Internal integrations use a simple bearer token (integration secret). Public integrations use OAuth2. Users must explicitly share pages/databases with the integration — nothing is accessible by default.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free plan allows API access with limited blocks and guests
pricing.notes: API access is available on all plans including free. Rate limits apply uniformly. Paid plans unlock larger block limits and advanced permissions.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Errors include a code and message field. Common errors like unauthorized or object_not_found are clear, but validation errors can be verbose and hard to parse.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency keys. Creating a page twice creates two pages. Agents must check for existing records before creating.
known_agent_gotchas: Rate limit of 3 req/s is very low — agents processing large databases will be severely throttled
Pages and databases must be explicitly shared with the integration — agents will get 404s on unshared content
Block children require separate API calls — fetching a full page requires N+1 requests
Notion blocks are highly nested — deep page structures require recursive fetching
No webhooks — agents cannot react to Notion changes without polling
Filter syntax for database queries is verbose and non-standard
